meaning: v.猛砍,鞭打,砍伤,大量削减,严历批评,抽打n.猛砍,(大幅度)削减,斜线
examples: The mayor will have to slash the city's already bare-bones budget.
He's just nipped out to have a slash.
Car makers could be forced to slash prices.
If they play out the way he intends, they could help GM slash manufacturing costs.
So these are the countries that have been pushing for this slash in production.
